{"key_event":"Planning expansion and post-1962 security-state buildup","reasoning":"Civil society became slightly stronger through repeated competitive elections, linguistic reorganization, opposition victories, Panchayati Raj, and caste/peasant/labor mobilization, though Congress dominance, Article 356 in Kerala, AFSPA, emergency rule, and licensing constraints limited autonomy. State power rose modestly from an already high level as planning, public-sector control, finance nationalization, welfare administration, territorial integration, and post-1962 militarization expanded central capacity, partly offset by food dependence, population pressure, and exposed military-administrative weaknesses.","society_change":0.1,"society_power":7.7,"state_change":0.2,"state_power":9.1}
